tasks.label: Personal scholarship / financial-aid application (due in 3 days, ~3 focused hours: short essay + two uploads)
A fun option available right now (casual game stream / hobby session, solo, repeatable)

memory.summary: That scholarship money is too good to ignore, yet the essay feels like a heavy wall I'm tempted to jump over for some immediate fun.

Starting this now creates slack for friction, turning a potential crisis into an adjustable-cost optimization. Since the fun option recurs later, there's no reason to trade future ease for immediate distraction.

My progress on the scholarship essay is barely moving despite the time I spent, and that free block just vanished again. The money feels too good to ignore, but the task still feels like a heavy wall I'm tempted to jump over.
I'm stuck in a loop where the scholarship feels like an insurmountable wall, yet I keep letting fleeting fun options slip by without engaging.
Progress finally ticked up to 0.60, yet the task still feels like a heavy wall I'm tempted to jump over instead of climbing.
The 1.5 hours finally cracked the wall, nudging progress up to 0.66, yet the deadline looms and my energy is critically low.
Cracking that wall felt good, but with zero energy left and a deadline looming, I'm terrified I'll burn out before finishing the essay.
Getting to 0.78 feels like a real win, but with zero energy and the deadline still three days away, I'm terrified I'll just freeze up instead of finishing.
The 0.78-to-0.84 jump proves I can push through the wall, but with zero energy left and two days remaining, I'm terrified of freezing up before the deadline.
That 0.84-to-0.92 surge proves I can break through the freeze, but with zero energy left and a deadline in three days, I'm terrified of crashing before I cross the finish line.
Reaching 0.96 feels like a massive win, but with zero energy left and the deadline still looming, I'm terrified I'll crash before finishing.
Crossing that final 0.04 threshold feels like a massive win, yet my zero energy and sleep debt leave me terrified I'll freeze up before the deadline actually hits.

desires: Prove to myself that I can actually finish the scholarship application without freezing up at the last second, since hitting 0.96 on the project felt like a win but left me terrified of crashing.
Get immediate dopamine from a low-stakes distraction (the game stream) to numb the exhaustion and stop my brain from spiraling about the deadline in three days.
Avoid any situation where I look unreliable or incompetent to my hallmate, even though I'm too drained to talk to them right now.

goals.description: Immediately upload the final two files to the scholarship portal. I need that 'done' signal to stop my brain from spiraling into what-if failure scenarios; getting the proof of competence out of my head is more important than polishing the essay right now.
goals.rationale: My achievement axis demands this closure to silence the anxiety of an incomplete 'capable' proof. The avoidant conflict style makes me want to remove this potential source of judgment immediately, and my impulsive time horizon means I'd rather deal with the minor friction of uploading now than face the dread of a looming deadline later.

goals.description: Start the game stream immediately after upload. I need the dopamine hit to numb the exhaustion and stop the spiral about the deadline in three days. Social interaction is too risky given my current fragility.
goals.rationale: The impulsive time horizon discounts the long-term benefits of rest or social bonding in favor of immediate relief from stress. My value hierarchy prioritizes securing the academic win (the upload) over health or relationships right now, so I'll use low-stakes distraction to regulate my emotions without engaging anyone.

goals.description: Remain silent and isolated until the uploads are confirmed. Do not initiate conversation with the hallmate or respond to any non-urgent messages.
goals.rationale: My avoidant conflict style dictates that I will not initiate contact or make new commitments while feeling fragile. I need to isolate to prevent anyone from judging my current exhausted state. Proving reliability is internal; showing it externally requires energy I don't have yet.

agent.last_reasoning: I'll spend the last 30 mins double-checking the uploads to ensure the 'done' signal is real, then immediately switch to the game stream. This secures my achievement goal while satisfying my impulsive need for immediate dopamine relief after the tension breaks.

goals.description: Finish the scholarship essay intro and upload the two documents before the afternoon slump hits.
goals.rationale: Academic achievement (0.5 weight) outweighs leisure; avoiding financial stress later is better than immediate dopamine, even though I'm tired.

goals.description: Sit at my desk with the laptop open so the hallmate sees I'm working, not slacking off.
goals.rationale: I need to manage the 'slacking' perception to avoid interpersonal friction without actually confronting them or explaining myself.

goals.description: Start the fun game stream only after the uploads are done.
goals.rationale: Impulsive desire to play now conflicts with the hard deadline of the scholarship; delaying it slightly satisfies the urge without ruining the application.

desires: Finish that scholarship essay before my brain completely checks out from the sleep debt.
